title: A Trend Factor for the Cross Section of Cryptocurrency Returns
venue: Journal of Financial and Quantitative Analysis (JFQA), Vol. 60(7), pp. 3116-3153
signal_or_research_question: CTREND: aggregate price and volume information across horizons into one trend signal
headline_finding: CTREND predicts crypto returns, is not subsumed by known factors, survives transaction costs, persists in large/liquid coins; an asset-pricing model with CTREND outperforms competing factor models
implementation_assumptions: Machine-learning aggregation of technical indicators; portfolio rebalance at the disclosed horizon
major_limitations: Sample up to 2024/2025; ML method needs transparent replication; not a simple baseline
direct_implication: Later challenger for MOM after simple momentum is frozen; do not substitute for primary momentum test

title: Crypto Carry
venue: Management Science (Articles in Advance, published online 6 May 2026)
universe_and_venues: Major cryptoassets; futures basis across venues
signal_or_research_question: Crypto carry: futures basis / past-return-driven carry across coins
headline_finding: Documented crypto carry premium; monthly returns large but collapse from +62% to -40% in stress; higher past crypto returns associate with larger crypto carry
implementation_assumptions: Futures-basis carry computed per coin; market/dollar neutrality
major_limitations: Stress reversal is severe; basis convergence risk; venue concentration
direct_implication: Split CARRY-01 into futures-basis, perpetual-funding, staking, emissions legs; treat as separate mechanisms

title: Cryptocurrency market risk-managed momentum strategies
universe_and_venues: Cryptocurrencies sorted into quintiles by 2-week return
headline_finding: Risk-managed momentum raises weekly return 3.18% to 3.47% and Sharpe 1.12 to 1.42; gains come from enhanced returns, not downside protection; crypto lacks extended momentum crashes
implementation_assumptions: 2-week formation, 1-week holding; value-weighted WML; Barroso-Santa-Clara scaling
major_limitations: Data from CoinMarketCap; short-sale constraints approximated; single signal
direct_implication: Report raw vs volatility-managed momentum separately; volatility management is a later challenger

title: Cryptocurrency momentum has (not) its moments
venue: Financial Markets and Portfolio Management, Vol. 39, pp. 443-476
signal_or_research_question: Tail behavior of crypto momentum; volatility-managed momentum crashes
headline_finding: Crypto momentum suffers severe crashes; even a single coin can make momentum insignificant; volatility management mitigates crashes; effect concentrated in large caps
implementation_assumptions: Equal-weighted large-cap momentum; volatility scaling per Barroso-Santa-Clara
major_limitations: Disagrees with LIT-026 on absence of crashes; large-cap focus
direct_implication: Require crash attribution and both raw and volatility-managed reporting; do not assume crashes are absent

title: Cross-sectional interactions in cryptocurrency returns
venue: International Review of Financial Analysis, Vol. 97, 103809
signal_or_research_question: Interaction effects among 40 return-predictive characteristics via double sorts
headline_finding: Numerous significant liquidity/risk/past-return interactions; OOS long-short Sharpe >1 (equal-weighted); low liquidity dampens trading
implementation_assumptions: 40 signals; equal- and value-weighted double sorts; BH correction
major_limitations: Equal-weighted OOS Sharpe >1 but tails thick; costs hurt short leg; liquidity dependence
direct_implication: Interaction/ML challengers only after transparent baselines; separate liquidity alpha from selection

title: Crypto factor zoo (.Zip)
venue: International Review of Financial Analysis, Vol. 113, 105137
universe_and_venues: Broad crypto cross-section; equal- and value-weighted portfolios
signal_or_research_question: Iterative alpha-based factor compression (Swade et al.) on crypto predictors
headline_finding: Only 2-3 factors beyond market eliminate all significant alphas; liquidity (turnover volatility, bid-ask) and blockchain-native (new-address-to-price) dominate; selections temporally unstable
implementation_assumptions: 36 factors; GRS iterative selection; transaction-cost sensitivity
major_limitations: Sample ends 2024; factor ranks shift across subperiods; costs narrow exploitable set
direct_implication: Raw long-short returns do not establish independent alpha; require latent/hidden-factor and accepted-crypto-factor controls

title: Token Dilution and the Cross-Section of Cryptocurrency Returns
venue: SSRN working paper (posted 24 Apr 2026; revised 20 May 2026)
signal_or_research_question: FDV premium and dilution rate as cross-sectional return predictors
headline_finding: FDV premium and 12-week dilution rate predict returns (t=-3.13, -2.79); long-short spreads 25-32% annualized; effect in first year of trading, absent in blue-chips/mature coins
implementation_assumptions: FDV premium, 12-week dilution rate; Newey-West t-stats; portfolio sorts
major_limitations: Working paper, unrefereed; sample ends 2026; effect concentrated in new/illiquid coins; limited-attention mechanism
direct_implication: Basis for new DIL-01 candidate; empirical test blocked until point-in-time supply/unlock data exist

title: Crypto Pricing with Hidden Factors
signal_or_research_question: Giglio-Xiu three-pass latent-factor asset pricing for crypto
headline_finding: Crypto returns load on crypto-specific and selected equity-industry (tech, profitability) factors; sentiment/Altcoin-Season/hacked-value state variables matter; latent controls change estimated premia materially
implementation_assumptions: Three-pass approach; weekly data; observed + latent factors
major_limitations: Working paper; one author; limited robustness detail on primary page
direct_implication: Require latent PCA / hidden-factor controls in all factor tests; premia can be biased without them

title: Crypto has Fundamentals: A Seven-Factor Model for Digital Asset Returns (Q-7)
signal_or_research_question: Seven-factor model: market/size/momentum + on-chain quality, value, residual volatility, funding sentiment
headline_finding: Five of six style factors significant; explains 63% per-token / 85% diversified OOS variance; reports short-term reversal not momentum in broader sample; declared conflict (QuantNest founder)
implementation_assumptions: Q-7 factors; sentiment regimes; OOS R-squared
major_limitations: Practitioner paper with commercial conflict; 90-token sample; reversal-vs-momentum divergence vs Liu et al.
direct_implication: Lead/challenger only; investigate reversal-vs-momentum divergence; not established evidence

authors: Li, X.; Liu, Z.; Liu, Y.; Zhu, S.; Yan, J.
title: Predicting cryptocurrency returns with machine learning: Evidence from high-dimensional factor modeling
signal_or_research_question: ML return prediction with 12 models; SHAP interpretability
headline_finding: Tree-based models (random forest) best; market-to-realized-value, new addresses, active addresses most influential; NN strategies highest cumulative gain
major_limitations: High-dimensional; gains may concentrate in illiquid assets; OOS stability across regimes unproven
direct_implication: Supports NET-01 candidate concepts; ML is a Phase-3 challenger after baselines

title: What data have told us about decentralized finance
universe_and_venues: DeFi protocols, DEX, lending, derivatives, governance
signal_or_research_question: Survey of DeFi empirical literature: liquidity, price discovery, leverage, fragility, MEV
headline_finding: DeFi replicates traditional finance with new risks from pseudonymity, smart contracts, composability; outlines open questions on efficiency, governance, systemic risk
direct_implication: Context for execution simulation and cost modeling of derivative/carry legs

title: The Economics of Liquid Staking Derivatives: Basis Determinants and Price Discovery
venue: Journal of Futures Markets, Vol. 45(2), pp. 91-117
sample_period: Lido staked ETH growth through 2023; basis analysis
universe_and_venues: Liquid staking tokens (e.g. stETH) vs underlying; Ethereum/Solana
signal_or_research_question: Determinants of the liquid-staking basis and price discovery
headline_finding: Basis wider when staking yield low, volatility high, secondary liquidity low; concentration risk widens basis; LSTs contribute growing price discovery
implementation_assumptions: Basis = derivative minus underlying; information shares
major_limitations: Single asset class (ETH LSTs); basis not identical to futures basis
direct_implication: Supports staking-rewards leg of CARRY; needs point-in-time staking-yield histories

authors: Mohammad, Z.; Harkiolakis, N.; Sarbazvatan, S.
title: Ethereum tokenomics and token value: a quantitative analysis of on-chain fundamentals (2021-2025)
signal_or_research_question: Relationship of TVL, unlocks, burns, gas, governance concentration to ETH price
headline_finding: TVL strongest price predictor (beta 0.8 long-run); token unlocks negative (beta -0.22, p<0.001); governance concentration insignificant
major_limitations: Single chain (Ethereum); high multicollinearity among burn/gas; correlation not causal
direct_implication: Reinforces dilution mechanism and the data-audit gate for DIL-01

title: The Two-Tiered Structure of Cryptocurrency Funding Rate Markets
signal_or_research_question: Funding-rate market integration, information flow, CEX-vs-DEX arbitrage
headline_finding: CEX dominate price discovery (61% higher integration); info flow CEX->DEX only; 17% of obs show >=20bp spreads but only 40% profitable after costs; forced exits in 95% of opportunities
implementation_assumptions: 35.7M one-minute funding obs; Granger causality; delta-neutral simulation
major_limitations: 8-day snapshot; high-frequency; not a factor study
direct_implication: Informs perpetual-funding leg of CARRY and realistic cost/turnover assumptions

authors: Han, C.; Kang, B.; Ryu, J.
title: Momentum in the Cryptocurrency Market: A Comprehensive Analysis under Realistic Assumptions
venue: SSRN working paper (posted 16 Jan 2024; last revised 26 Mar 2026)
signal_or_research_question: Time-series vs cross-sectional momentum under realistic implementation assumptions
headline_finding: Time-series and cross-sectional momentum must be evaluated separately; under daily price fluctuations many momentum portfolios are liquidated before terminal backtest returns and portfolios with statistically significant mean returns often earn negative profits; mean-return significance is insufficient evidence of realizable profitability; time-series momentum strong, cross-sectional momentum almost non-existent
implementation_assumptions: Daily mark-to-market wealth paths; explicit margin/futures mechanics and liquidation assumptions; separate long and short leg attribution
major_limitations: Working paper, unrefereed; pre-2025 sample; numerical results not independently re-verified here
direct_implication: Primary source for momentum implementation design: separate TS/CS diagnostics, transaction costs, liquidation/realizable-profit checks, explicit margin/futures assumptions
